title: Addressing v2: uniform path-based addresses; rule/gate as addressable items
description:
Epic `90a2dbfd` (declarative kinds over sources, done) made declaring a new kind pure config: REQ-02 added a config-declared toml source descriptor and REQ-04 made `jit init` author the `[item_kinds]` table. Its REQ-07 (rule/gate as addressable items) was deferred because rule self-ids carry colons that collide with the label `namespace:value` separator.

Investigation reopened that thread and grew it into an addressing redesign. The real blocker is not the colon: `@` is not a legal label-value character today (`crates/jit/src/labels.rs:24`), so project-scoped links (`enforces:@/...`) cannot be authored at all. This epic introduces one uniform, colon-free, path-based address scheme over the project, makes `rule` and `gate` addressable kinds over toml sources, rebinds `enforced-by` to real items, renders a rules/gates reference, re-addresses all existing kinds, and lands the multi-jit address form (resolution deferred).

Full motivation, grounded findings, the converged design, the target grammar, and every open point to settle in planning are in the linked design brief `dev/studies/addressing-v2-rule-gate-items.md`. The open points there are decisions for the formal jit-plan; this epic states the end-state requirements.

## Success Criteria

- REQ-01 [hard] One uniform address scheme is minted, parsed, and resolved: project items `@[<project>]/<kind>/<self-id>`, issue items `@/issue/<short-id>/<kind>/<self-id>`, with `<short-id>/<self-id>` as sugar expanding to the issue form. The colon `:` is reserved solely as the label `namespace:value` separator and is never overloaded inside a value. Verified by parser/resolution tests over project and issue scopes plus the sugar form.
- REQ-02 [hard] The label-value grammar admits a leading `@` and `@<project>` and the deeper `/`-path form; the single-colon split (`splitn(2, ':')`) is retained; every site that re-splits a value is audited so the wider grammar breaks no parsing or resolution. Verified by grammar tests covering accepted/rejected values.
- REQ-03 [hard] `rule` is an addressable kind sourced from `rules.toml` via the config toml source descriptor; rule self-ids carry no colon (the origin such as `default`/`bracket` moves out of the id into a field). Verified by indexing rules as items and resolving `@/rule/<self-id>`.
- REQ-04 [hard] `gate` is an addressable kind; `gates.json` is migrated to `gates.toml` with schema parity (key, title, description, stage, mode, checker, priority, and any prompt/env fields); no consumer still reads `gates.json`. Verified by gate operations running over the toml source and resolving `@/gate/<key>`.
- REQ-05 [hard] Every invariant `enforced-by` binding resolves to a real addressable item (`@/rule/...` or `@/gate/...`); `invariants.toml` bindings are rebound to the new addresses. Verified by a resolution check over the seed registry that fails on any unresolved binding.
- REQ-06 [hard] Work can be linked via `enforces:@/<rule-or-gate>` and that link resolves like `satisfies:`/`per:`/`mitigates:` today. Verified by authoring such a label and resolving it to the target item.
- REQ-07 [hard] A rules/gates reference document is rendered from the registry (analogous to the CLAUDE.md invariant projection), reusing the projection style mechanism. Verified by a render test producing the reference from the registry.
- REQ-08 [hard] All existing kinds are re-addressed uniformly (invariant, requirement, decision, risk, definition) and this repo's existing qualified-id links, labels, docs, and `.jit/` data are migrated; `jit validate` is green after migration. Verified by a green validate plus a scan showing no legacy-form addresses remain.
- REQ-09 [hard] The multi-jit address form is accepted now with resolution deferred: the grammar/parser accept `@<project>`; only the local project resolves; a non-local reference is parse-valid and returns a clear not-resolvable error. No federation or project registry is built. Verified by tests over local resolution and the non-local error path.

## Out of Scope

- Cross-jit federation / actual remote resolution (only the address form lands; resolution is local-only).
- Re-introducing enforcement-drift accounting (epic `90a2dbfd` REQ-05 removed it; it stays removed).
- Value injection / codegen: jit makes rules/gates addressable and citable, it does not rewrite the code that physically holds a value.
